matter.title: Helvetia Cloud × Northstar Retail SaaS Agreement

expectations.uptime: 99.5% monthly uptime measured by supplier telemetry; planned maintenance excluded; credits are the exclusive SLA remedy.
expectations.liability: Consequential loss excluded; aggregate liability capped at CHF 120,000, including outage-related claims.
expectations.legal_architecture: Swiss substantive law, Zurich seat, Swiss Rules, one arbitrator, proceedings in English.

expectations.uptime: 99.5% measured from customer-facing availability; maintenance counts unless notified 72 hours ahead; credits accumulate for each complete 0.1-point shortfall.
expectations.liability: The limitation should not protect intentional or grossly negligent conduct; the legal effect requires targeted review.
expectations.legal_architecture: Initially expected English law and London; requires an explicit choice rather than a hybrid legal configuration.

clauses.title: Availability, evidence, and service credits
clauses.sourcePassage: Supplier will provide 99.5% monthly uptime, excluding scheduled maintenance. Service credits apply as set out in the Order Form.
clauses.proposedLanguage: If actual monthly uptime falls below 99.5%, Customer receives 5% of the monthly fee for each complete 0.1 percentage-point shortfall, capped at 100% of the monthly fee.

clauses.title: Consequential loss and liability cap
clauses.sourcePassage: Neither party is liable for indirect or consequential loss. Supplier's aggregate liability is capped at CHF 120,000.
clauses.proposedLanguage: Indirect and consequential loss is excluded and aggregate liability is capped at CHF 120,000, subject to liabilities that cannot validly be excluded or limited. Targeted professional review is required before signature.

clauses.title: Governing law and arbitration
clauses.sourcePassage: This Agreement is governed by Swiss law. Disputes shall be finally resolved by arbitration in Zurich.
clauses.proposedLanguage: Swiss substantive law; Swiss Rules; one arbitrator; Zurich seat; English language; mandatory laws applying irrespective of party choice are reserved.

findings.title: The parties measure and price the same uptime promise differently
findings.supplierPosition: Supplier telemetry controls and the draft does not define a stepped credit formula.
findings.customerPosition: Customer-facing evidence should control and each complete 0.1-point shortfall should produce a 5% credit.
findings.consequence: At 99.2% uptime, the remedy cannot be reproduced unless measurement, exclusions, step size, rate, fee, and cap are explicit.
findings.uncertainty: The draft does not define the evidence hierarchy or complete-step calculation.

options.label: Executable stepped credit
options.tradeoff: Creates a reproducible remedy from shared evidence.
options.language: Monthly uptime is determined by the jointly visible external monitor. Scheduled maintenance is excluded only with 72 hours' notice and is capped at four hours monthly. If uptime falls below 99.5%, Customer receives 5% of the monthly fee for each complete 0.1 percentage-point shortfall, capped at 100% of the monthly fee.

options.label: Fixed 10% shortfall credit
options.tradeoff: Simpler remedy but less sensitive to outage severity.
options.language: Monthly uptime is determined by the jointly visible external monitor. If uptime falls below 99.5%, Customer receives a fixed credit equal to 10% of the monthly fee, capped at that amount.

findings.title: The liability cap creates a targeted mandatory-law review question
findings.supplierPosition: The CHF 120,000 cap should apply to all outage-related claims.
findings.customerPosition: The cap should not protect intentional or grossly negligent conduct.
findings.consequence: The selected legal architecture may affect the validity and interpretation of advance limitations; the system cannot determine enforceability.
findings.uncertainty: Targeted professional review is required before signature.

options.label: CHF 120,000 cap with mandatory-law reservation
options.tradeoff: Preserves commercial certainty without asserting enforceability.
options.language: Indirect and consequential loss is excluded and aggregate liability is capped at CHF 120,000, subject to liabilities that cannot validly be excluded or limited. The effect of intentional or grossly negligent conduct requires targeted professional review.
unresolvedMatters: Whether intentional or grossly negligent conduct affects the agreed limitation under applicable mandatory law.

findings.title: The parties assumed different legal and procedural architectures
findings.supplierPosition: Swiss substantive law and Zurich arbitration under the Swiss Rules.
findings.customerPosition: English law and London were initially assumed.
findings.consequence: The parties must choose one explicit architecture; ZIAAP does not blend the systems.
findings.uncertainty: Mandatory laws applying irrespective of party choice remain reserved.

options.label: Swiss law + Zurich seat
options.tradeoff: One clean architecture with English-language proceedings.
options.language: This Agreement is governed by Swiss substantive law. Disputes are finally resolved under the Swiss Rules of International Arbitration by one arbitrator seated in Zurich, Switzerland, in English. Mandatory laws applying irrespective of party choice are reserved.

sources.title: Draft SaaS Agreement, clause 4.2
sources.passage: 99.5% monthly uptime; service credits to be specified.

sources.title: Swiss Code of Obligations, Article 100
sources.passage: Advance exclusion or limitation for intentional or grossly negligent conduct raises a targeted statutory constraint requiring professional review.

sources.title: Swiss Arbitration Centre Model Clause
sources.passage: The model clause separately specifies rules, number of arbitrators, seat, and language.

metadata.label: Cached verified fallback
metadata.sourceCoverage: Three bounded divergence questions with explicit source and review status.

decisions.language: Monthly uptime is determined by the jointly visible external monitor. Scheduled maintenance is excluded only with 72 hours' notice and is capped at four hours monthly. If uptime falls below 99.5%, Customer receives 5% of the monthly fee for each complete 0.1 percentage-point shortfall, capped at 100% of the monthly fee.

Indirect and consequential loss is excluded and aggregate liability is capped at CHF 120,000, subject to liabilities that cannot validly be excluded or limited. The effect of intentional or grossly negligent conduct requires targeted professional review.
unresolvedMatters: Whether intentional or grossly negligent conduct affects the agreed limitation under applicable mandatory law.

decisions.language: This Agreement is governed by Swiss substantive law. Disputes are finally resolved under the Swiss Rules of International Arbitration by one arbitrator seated in Zurich, Switzerland, in English. Mandatory laws applying irrespective of party choice are reserved.

legalConstraint.query: Swiss Code of Obligations Article 100 advance limitation intentional or gross negligence
legalConstraint.headline: Legal constraint identified

source.title: Swiss Code of Obligations, Article 100
source.passage: Advance exclusion or limitation for intentional or grossly negligent conduct raises a targeted statutory constraint requiring professional review.

legalConstraint.reviewInstruction: Targeted professional review required before signature.
legalConstraint.conclusion: No enforceability conclusion is made. The constraint is isolated for qualified review.

residualReviewPacket.issue: Whether the supplier's conduct amounts to gross negligence and affects the agreed limitation of liability.
residualReviewPacket.supplierPosition: The agreed CHF 120,000 cap applies, subject to mandatory law.
residualReviewPacket.customerPosition: Grossly negligent conduct should not receive the benefit of the limitation.

ledger.detail: Validated fixture loaded; no live analysis implied.
